questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Equal masses of ethane and hydrogen gas are present in a container at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The fraction of the total pressure exerted by ethane gas is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
The van der Waals equation for one mole of a real gas can be written as $\left(\mathrm{P}+\mathrm{a} / \mathrm{V}^2\right)(\mathrm{V}-\mathrm{b})=\mathrm{RT}$. For the gases $\mathrm{H}_2, \mathrm{NH}_3$, and $\mathrm{CH}_4$, the value of ' a ' $\left(\right.$ bar $\mathrm{L}^{-2} \mathrm{mol}^{-2}$ ) are $0.2453,4.170$ and 2.253 respectively.
Which of the following can be inferred from the 'a' values?
options: value of 'a' for $\mathrm{CH}_4$ is less than that of $\mathrm{NH}_3$ because it has the lower molar mass
intermolecular forces are the strongest in hydrogen

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Which of the following will not give a straight line plot for an ideal gas?

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: The pressure inside two gas cylinders of volume $25 \mathrm{~m}^3$ and $50 \mathrm{~m}^3$ are 10 kPa and 20 kPa respectively. The cylinders are kept at the same temperature and sepearted by a valve. What is the pressure in the combined system when the valve is opened?

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: When a sample of gas kept at $20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and 4.0 atm is heated to $40^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ at constant volume
options: average speed of the gas molecules will decrease
number of collisions between the gas molecules per second will remain the same.
average kinetic energy of the gas will increase.
pressure of the gas will become 8 atm .

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Which of the following is correct?
A liquid with
options: low vapour pressure will have a low surface tension and high boiling point
high vapour pressure will have high intermolecular forces and high boiling point
low vapour pressure will have high surface tension and high boiling point
low vapour pressure will have low surface tension and low boiling point

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
A substance $\mathbf{X}$ was heated at constant pressure and the temperature observed at various times of heating was plotted as given below
Which of the following is/are correct? I. Melting point of $\mathbf{X}$ is $-5^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ II. Solid and liquid forms of $\mathbf{X}$ coexist in the region b III. Boiling point of $\mathbf{X}$ is $55^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ IV. Solid and liquid forms of $\mathbf{X}$ coexist in the region d

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: A helium cylinder in which the volume of gas $=2.24 \mathrm{~L}$ at STP ( $1 \mathrm{~atm}, 273 \mathrm{~K}$ ) developed a leak and when the leak was plugged the pressure in the cylinder was seen to have dropped to 550 mm of Hg . The number of moles of He gas that had escaped due to this leak is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: One mole of neon (atomic mass $=20 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ ) and one mole of argon (atomic mass $=40 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ ) are stored in two separate containers I and II, at temperature T and 2 T respectively. If both the gases are assumed to behave ideally
options: K.E. and average velocity of the gas molecules will be the same in both I and II
K.E. and average velocity of the gas molecules in II will be twice that of the gas molecules in I
K.E. of the gas molecules in II will be twice that in I and average velocity of the gas molecules in both I and II will be the same
Both K.E. and average velocity of the gas molecules in I will be twice that of the gas molecules in II

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: A closed 2.0 L container initially holds $3.0 \mathrm{~mol}^{\circ}$ of $\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ and $2.0 \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{of}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ at room temperature, T. If the pressure remains constant when 1.0 mol of $\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ is added, the final temperature of the system is-(Assume ideal gas behaviour throughout)

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
A given amount of an ideal gas undergoes the cyclic process ABCA as given below.
The equivalent representation/s of the same process is/are

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Among the following, the correct statements about the compressibility factor $(Z)$ of real gases are:
(i) If $\mathrm{Z} < 1$, intermolecular repulsive forces are more dominant.
(ii) If $\mathrm{Z} < 1$, intermolecular attractive forces are more dominant.
(iii) If $Z>1$, intermolecular repulsive forces are more dominant.
(iv) If $Z>1$, intermolecular attractive forces are more dominant.

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Two flasks I and II of equal volume are evacuated and connected by a tube of negligible volume fitted with a stopcock. They are then placed in two different constant temperature baths of 250 K and 750 K respectively. 20 moles of an ideal gas are introduced into the system of these flasks through the stopcock. When the system reaches equilibrium, the ratio of the moles of the gas in flasks I and II is:
